title: 10 best herbal teas for eczema
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eczema are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate eczema symptoms. These natural remedies offer an alternative to traditional medications and can improve the quality of life for those suffering from eczema. Some popular herbal teas known to help include chamomile, calendula, and nettle. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin, while calendula helps promote skin healing and nettle boasts antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects. By drinking these teas, individuals may experience reduced redness, itching, and inflammation, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without constant discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and swelling. The tea is rich in antioxidants that help to repair damaged skin tissue and promote the growth of new skin cells. Yarrow tea also contains salicylic acid, which can help to exfoliate dead skin cells and unclog pores, preventing the buildup of toxins that can trigger eczema outbreaks. Additionally, yarrow tea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help to prevent flare-ups and reduce the severity of eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce eczema-related redness, swelling, and discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tea, from volatile oils like azulene and camphor, can help prevent skin infections and support eczema wound healing.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent qualities, caused by tannins, help control eczema-related itching, oozing, and skin irritation by constricting and protecting the affected area.
Antipruritic: The antipruritic properties of yarrow tea, due to alkaloids and lactones, help alleviate itching and reduce the urge to scratch, which is beneficial for eczema sufferers.
Wound-healing: Yarrow tea's wound-healing abilities, from the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and tannins, can help soothe and promote the healing of eczema-related skin damage and lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe eczema symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that can support skin health and reduce eczema flare-ups.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that can help moisturize and nourish dry, itchy eczema skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it sit for 1-2 minutes to cool slightly.
Place the yarrow in a tea infuser and steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of yarrow tea daily, between meals, for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and irritation of the skin. It contains mucilage, a gel-like substance that creates a protective barrier on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy patches. Additionally, marshmallow tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and promote skin healing. The tea can be consumed internally or applied topically as a compress to affected areas, making it a versatile and natural remedy for eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, which helps reduce inflammation and soothes irritated skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms.
Demulcent: The mucilage in marshmallow tea also creates a protective barrier on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and alleviate dryness associated with eczema.
Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tea has antimicrobial properties due to its tannin content, which can help prevent infection in eczema-prone skin.
Immune-boosting: The tea's antioxidant and vitamin content supports the immune system, promoting overall skin health and reducing eczema flare-ups.
Soothing: Marshmallow tea contains flavonoids that have a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, redness, and irritation caused by eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, marshmallow tea leaves can help soothe irritated skin and reduce eczema symptoms.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, marshmallow tea flowers can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing in eczema-prone skin.
Roots: Containing mucilage, marshmallow tea roots can help moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin associated with eczema.
Seeds: High in flavonoids, marshmallow tea seeds can help reduce inflammation and alleviate eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers, let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and let it cool before drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin condition. Burdock contains essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that can help soothe irritated skin, reduce redness, and promote skin healing. The herb's natural detoxifying properties can also help eliminate toxins from the body, which can contribute to eczema flare-ups. Additionally, burdock tea has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help prevent skin infections that can exacerbate eczema. Drinking burdock tea regularly can help manage eczema symptoms and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea contains active constituents like phenolic acids and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation in eczema by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in burdock tea, due to its rich supply of polyphenols and flavonoids, aids in neutralizing free radicals, thereby promoting skin cell health and reducing oxidative stress associated with eczema.

Antimicrobial: Burdock tea has antimicrobial properties, owing to its arctiin and lignan content, that help control the growth of Staphylococcus aureus, a bacterium commonly found on eczema-prone skin, reducing the risk of infection.

Diuretic: Burdock tea's diuretic effect, attributed to its potassium, magnesium, and calcium content, helps eliminate toxins and excess fluids from the body, supporting overall skin health and reducing eczema symptoms.

Immune-boosting: Burdock tea contains essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and E, that support the immune system, which is crucial for individuals with eczema as it helps combat the immune dysfunction associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in medicinal tea for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties.
Leaves: Burdock leaves are rich in essential nutrients and have been known to soothe irritated skin, making them beneficial for eczema relief.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain beneficial oils and antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and improve skin health in eczema patients.
Flowers: Burdock flowers have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of eczema and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of burdock tea daily, between meals, for best results in managing eczema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritated skin and promote healing. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. Drinking pot marigold tea can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema, while also boosting the immune system and providing antioxidant benefits. Additionally, the tea can be used topically as a compress or added to bath water to provide direct relief to affected areas of the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation in eczema due to the presence of flavonoids, which inhibit the release of inflammatory chemicals.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the compound thujone, help prevent skin infections that can occur in eczema-prone skin.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for eczema-related lesions, due to the presence of triterpenes and flavonoids.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, provided by carotenoids and flavonoids, protects the skin from oxidative stress, supporting overall skin health in eczema.
Immune-modulating: Its immune-modulating effects, attributed to the active constituents like saponins and polysaccharides, help regulate the immune response, potentially reducing eczema flare-ups.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers help soothe irritated skin and reduce eczema symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have skin-healing and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for eczema relief.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which help to maintain skin moisture and reduce eczema-related dryness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marigold flowers (Calendula officinalis).
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. It is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. White tea also has antibacterial properties that can help prevent skin infections, which are common in people with eczema. Additionally, white tea has been shown to improve skin hydration and elasticity, which can help alleviate the dry, scaly skin associated with eczema. Overall, white tea can help soothe and heal eczema-prone skin, providing relief from itching, irritation, and other symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with eczema, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, supporting skin health and healing in eczema-prone skin, due to the presence of catechins and epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Antimicrobial: White tea exhibits antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of infection in eczema-affected areas, attributed to its tannins, caffeine, and essential oils.

Immune-boosting: White tea helps strengthen the immune system, promoting overall skin health and reducing the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups, as it contains high levels of antioxidants and amino acids that support immune function.

Soothing and calming: White tea has calming properties that can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can trigger eczema outbreaks, due to theanine and other amino acids that promote relaxation and mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help reduce inflammation and soothe eczema symptoms.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, white tea buds can help alleviate skin irritation and redness associated with eczema.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, white tea flowers can help promote wound healing and provide relief from eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and let it cool to 170-185°F, the optimal temperature for white tea, for 5-10 minutes.
Steep 1 teaspoon of high-quality, organic white tea leaves and 1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers in the hot water for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of calendula petals and 1 teaspoon of licorice root to the mixture, allowing the herbs to steep for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Strain the tea, separating the liquid from the solid plant material.
Drink the herbal white tea up to three times a day, ideally between meals, for its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects on eczema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Chamomile contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help decrease inflammation and promote healing. The tea can be applied topically as a compress or ingested orally to help from the inside out. Drinking chamomile tea can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger eczema flare-ups. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, which can help alleviate the itching and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the anti-inflammatory compound chamazulene, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation in eczema-prone skin.
Antimicrobial: The tea's antimicrobial properties, due to its content of active constituents like α-bisabolol and matricin, help prevent skin infections that can occur in eczema-affected areas.
Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, such as apigenin and luteolin, which help protect the skin from oxidative stress and support skin health in eczema patients.
Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its flavonoids and essential oils, which can help eczema-related skin damage and lesions.
Soothing and calming: German chamomile tea has a soothing effect on the skin, primarily due to its azulene content, providing relief from itching, redness, and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help soothe irritated skin and alleviate eczema symptoms.
Leaves: Containing therapeutic compounds, chamomile leaves contribute to reducing inflammation and providing relief for eczema-prone skin.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, chamomile seeds aid in promoting skin healing and provide relief from itchiness caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain out the chamomile flowers and discard them.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation of the skin. It contains antioxidants that help to fight free radicals, which can damage skin cells and worsen eczema symptoms. Chicory tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, vitamin C, and potassium, which are essential for healthy skin. Furthermore, chicory tea has natural cooling and soothing effects, which can provide relief from the itching and discomfort associated with eczema. Drinking chicory tea regularly can help to strengthen the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall skin health, making it an effective natural remedy for eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which help reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including polyphenols and flavonoids, in chicory tea helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and promotes skin health.
Immune-modulating: Chicory tea has immune-modulating properties due to its polysaccharides and polyphenols, which can help regulate the immune response and alleviate eczema symptoms.
Antimicrobial: Chicory tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, mainly due to its rosmarinic acid content, which can help prevent skin infections that can worsen eczema.
Soothing: Chicory tea contains mucilaginous compounds that create a soothing and protective film on the skin, helping to alleviate itching, dryness, and irritation caused by eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory tea made from the roots is often used as a traditional remedy for eczema due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Chicory leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been known to help alleviate skin irritation and reduce redness associated with eczema.
Flowers: Chicory flowers contain beneficial compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making them a popular choice for eczema tea.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried chicory root, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, cover, and let steep for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the chicory root, then let it cool down.
Drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea daily for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with eczema because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate skin inflammation, redness, and irritation. Horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that can improve skin elasticity and strengthen connective tissues, which may help reduce the severity of eczema symptoms. Additionally, horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections caused by scratching and picking at eczema-prone skin. Drinking horsetail tea may also boost the immune system, promoting overall skin health and reducing the frequency and duration of eczema flare-ups. However, it is essential to note that while horsetail tea may provide some relief for eczema symptoms, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in eczema-prone skin. This property is important for eczema as it alleviates redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the condition.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea is rich in antioxidants, including silica and selenium, that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals. This property is crucial for eczema as it aids in maintaining skin health and promoting faster healing.

Astringent: Horsetail tea has astringent properties due to its tannin content, which helps tighten and soothe inflamed skin. This property is significant for eczema as it can help relieve itching and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, thanks to compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, that help prevent bacterial and fungal infections. This property is vital for eczema as it reduces the risk of secondary infections that can exacerbate the condition.

Nutrient-rich: Horsetail tea contains essential minerals, such as silica, calcium, and magnesium, which support skin health and regeneration. This property is important for eczema as it contributes to strengthening the skin barrier and promoting overall skin resilience.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of the horsetail tea plant is used in medicinal tea for eczema due to its high silica content, which can help to improve skin health and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating eczema symptoms such as redness, itching, and irritation.
Stems: The stems of the horsetail tea plant contain bioactive compounds that can help to strengthen skin tissue, promote wound healing, and alleviate eczema-related skin conditions.
Seeds: The seeds of the horsetail tea plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and maintain healthy skin, making them beneficial for eczema treatment.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb, simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, cover, and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the horsetail herb and discard it.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the horsetail tea daily for eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation of the skin. It also contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, fennel tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections, which are common in people with eczema. Furthermore, fennel tea can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger eczema flare-ups. The tea's soothing and calming effects can help alleviate itching and discomfort, providing relief to those suffering from eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, help reduce skin redness, swelling, and irritation associated with eczema.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of fennel tea, resulting from flavonoids and phenolic acids, aid in protecting skin cells from oxidative stress and support overall skin health in eczema-prone individuals.

Antimicrobial: Fennel tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds, help maintain a healthy skin microbiome and protect against potential skin infections in individuals with eczema.

Immune-modulating: Fennel tea's immune-modulating properties, attributed to its rich polysaccharide and flavonoid content, help regulate immune responses and reduce the severity and frequency of eczema flare-ups.

Diaphoretic: Fennel tea's diaphoretic properties, resulting from its volatile oils, promote perspiration and help eliminate toxins from the body, supporting overall skin health and reducing eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with eczema.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain essential oils and rosmarinic acid, known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, beneficial for eczema-prone skin.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in soothing skin irritations and reducing redness caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water, then let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of fennel tea daily, preferably between meals, for best results in managing eczema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with eczema because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties. These properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. Additionally, licorice tea may help to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, further alleviating eczema symptoms. Furthermore, licorice tea has been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which can help to prevent skin infections that can exacerbate eczema. Drinking licorice tea may also help to hydrate and soothe dry, irritated skin, providing additional relief for individuals with eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea contains glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation in the skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms.
Immune system support: The flavonoids in licorice tea boost the immune system, helping the body fight off eczema triggers.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in licorice tea, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema.
Antimicrobial effects: Licorice tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent skin infections that can occur as a result of eczema.
Soothing properties: The flavonoids in licorice tea have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing itchiness and helping to heal eczema-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, the roots of the licorice tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, the leaves of the licorice tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for eczema as they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: Containing essential oil and glycyrrhizin, the seeds of the licorice tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for eczema as they have soothing and moisturizing properties.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the tea, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of licorice tea daily, as needed, to help soothe eczema symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eczema with herbal teas is by regularly consuming chamomile or calendula tea, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin. Additionally, drinking nettle tea can provide relief from eczema symptoms due to its high mineral and nutrient content. Always consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating herbal teas into your treatment plan.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal teas include psoriasis and dermatitis. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe irritated skin. Green tea is rich in polyphenols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin hydration. Nettle tea is also known to alleviate symptoms of skin conditions due to its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies.
